classification.attack_type: Authorization Bypass -- Missing per-client visibility filter on user subresource endpoints
classification.novel_sub_variant_description: Original CVE-2026-11986 was role assign/unassign asymmetry; this is missing auth.clients().canView() filter on UserResource session/consent/offline-session response builders -- same Class 10 root cause, different code surface (user subresource endpoints vs role-mapper endpoints)

signals.confirmation_signal: HTTP 200 on GET /users/{id}/sessions returns hidden client UUID and clientId in clients map to view-users-only actor; GET /users/{id}/consents returns hidden client consent record; GET /users/{id}/offline-sessions/{clientUuid} returns full offline session data. Direct GET /clients/{id} correctly returns 403 for same actor.

retrieval_summary.summary: Information disclosure via user subresource Admin API endpoints: view-users-only delegated admin enumerates hidden client UUIDs, clientIds, session participation, offline-token grants, and consent metadata through GET /users/{id}/sessions, /consents, /offline-sessions/{clientUuid}. Missing auth.clients().canView(client) filter in UserResource response builders. Confirmed on Keycloak latest (dev branch). Novel sub-variant of FGAP authorization asymmetry (Class 10) -- different code surface from #50983 (auth-flow endpoint) and original CVE-2026-11986 (role assignment).
